content:
## All 71 Python Built-in Functions

Python has exactly 71 built-in functions natively available. Here is the complete list:

- **`abs()`**: Returns absolute value
- **`aiter()`**: Returns asynchronous iterator (Python 3.10+)
- **`all()`**: Returns True if all elements are true
- **`anext()`**: Returns next item from async iterator (Python 3.10+)
- **`any()`**: Returns True if any element is true
- **`ascii()`**: Returns string containing printable representation
- **`bin()`**: Converts integer to binary string
- **`bool()`**: Returns boolean value
- **`breakpoint()`**: Drops into the debugger
- **`bytearray()`**: Returns a mutable bytearray object
- **`bytes()`**: Returns an immutable bytes object
- **`callable()`**: Checks if object appears callable
- **`chr()`**: Returns character from Unicode integer
- **`classmethod()`**: Converts a method into a class method
- **`compile()`**: Compiles source into code object
- **`complex()`**: Creates a complex number
- **`delattr()`**: Deletes an attribute from an object
- **`dict()`**: Creates a dictionary
- **`dir()`**: Returns list of valid attributes of an object
- **`divmod()`**: Returns quotient and remainder
- **`enumerate()`**: Yields index and value pairs
- **`eval()`**: Evaluates a Python expression
- **`exec()`**: Executes dynamic Python code
- **`filter()`**: Filters elements from an iterable
- **`float()`**: Converts to a floating point number
- **`format()`**: Formats a value
- **`frozenset()`**: Creates an immutable frozenset
- **`getattr()`**: Returns the value of an attribute
- **`globals()`**: Returns dictionary of current global symbol table
- **`hasattr()`**: Checks if object has given attribute
- **`hash()`**: Returns the hash value of an object
- **`help()`**: Invokes the built-in help system
- **`hex()`**: Converts integer to hexadecimal string
- **`id()`**: Returns the identity (memory address) of an object
- **`input()`**: Reads a line from input
- **`int()`**: Converts to an integer
- **`isinstance()`**: Checks if an object is an instance of a class
- **`issubclass()`**: Checks if a class is a subclass of another
- **`iter()`**: Returns an iterator object
- **`len()`**: Returns the length (number of items)
- **`list()`**: Creates a list
- **`locals()`**: Returns dictionary of current local symbol table
- **`map()`**: Applies function to every item of an iterable
- **`max()`**: Returns the largest item
- **`memoryview()`**: Returns a memory view object
- **`min()`**: Returns the smallest item
- **`next()`**: Retrieves the next item from an iterator
- **`object()`**: Returns a new featureless object
- **`oct()`**: Converts integer to octal string
- **`open()`**: Opens a file and returns a stream
- **`ord()`**: Returns an integer representing the Unicode char
- **`pow()`**: Returns base to the power exp
- **`print()`**: Prints to the standard output
- **`property()`**: Returns a property attribute
- **`range()`**: Generates a sequence of numbers
- **`repr()`**: Returns a string containing printable representation
- **`reversed()`**: Returns a reverse iterator
- **`round()`**: Rounds a number
- **`set()`**: Creates a set
- **`setattr()`**: Sets the value of an attribute
- **`slice()`**: Returns a slice object representing a set of indices
- **`sorted()`**: Returns a new sorted list
- **`staticmethod()`**: Converts a method to a static method
- **`str()`**: Returns a string version of an object
- **`sum()`**: Sums the items of an iterable
- **`super()`**: Returns a proxy object that delegates method calls to a parent or sibling class
- **`tuple()`**: Creates a tuple
- **`type()`**: Returns the type of an object
- **`vars()`**: Returns the __dict__ attribute for a module, class, instance
- **`zip()`**: Iterates over several iterables in parallel
- **`__import__()`**: Invoked by the import statement (rarely used directly)
codeExample:
# Reference guide for all 71 Python built-in functions

# abs(): Returns absolute value
abs(-5)

# aiter(): Returns asynchronous iterator (Python 3.10+)
aiter(async_iterable)

# all(): Returns True if all elements are true
all([True, False])

# anext(): Returns next item from async iterator (Python 3.10+)
anext(async_iterator)

# any(): Returns True if any element is true
any([False, True])

# ascii(): Returns string containing printable representation
ascii(obj)

# bin(): Converts integer to binary string
bin(10)

# bool(): Returns boolean value
bool(1)

# breakpoint(): Drops into the debugger
breakpoint()

# bytearray(): Returns a mutable bytearray object
bytearray([50, 100])

# bytes(): Returns an immutable bytes object
bytes([50, 100])

# callable(): Checks if object appears callable
callable(print)

# chr(): Returns character from Unicode integer
chr(65)

# classmethod(): Converts a method into a class method
@classmethod

# compile(): Compiles source into code object
compile("x=5", "test", "exec")

# complex(): Creates a complex number
complex(1, 2)

# delattr(): Deletes an attribute from an object
delattr(obj, "name")

# dict(): Creates a dictionary
dict(a=1, b=2)

# dir(): Returns list of valid attributes of an object
dir()

# divmod(): Returns quotient and remainder
divmod(10, 3)

# enumerate(): Yields index and value pairs
list(enumerate(["a", "b"]))

# eval(): Evaluates a Python expression
eval("5 + 10")

# exec(): Executes dynamic Python code
exec("print('Hi')")

# filter(): Filters elements from an iterable
list(filter(None, [0, 1]))

# float(): Converts to a floating point number
float("3.14")

# format(): Formats a value
format(3.1415, ".2f")

# frozenset(): Creates an immutable frozenset
frozenset([1, 2, 3])

# getattr(): Returns the value of an attribute
getattr(obj, "name")

# globals(): Returns dictionary of current global symbol table
globals()

# hasattr(): Checks if object has given attribute
hasattr(obj, "name")

# hash(): Returns the hash value of an object
hash("test")

# help(): Invokes the built-in help system
help(print)

# hex(): Converts integer to hexadecimal string
hex(255)

# id(): Returns the identity (memory address) of an object
id(obj)

# input(): Reads a line from input
input("Prompt:")

# int(): Converts to an integer
int("10")

# isinstance(): Checks if an object is an instance of a class
isinstance(5, int)

# issubclass(): Checks if a class is a subclass of another
issubclass(bool, int)

# iter(): Returns an iterator object
iter([1, 2])

# len(): Returns the length (number of items)
len("hello")

# list(): Creates a list
list((1, 2))

# locals(): Returns dictionary of current local symbol table
locals()

# map(): Applies function to every item of an iterable
list(map(str, [1, 2]))

# max(): Returns the largest item
max(1, 5, 3)

# memoryview(): Returns a memory view object
memoryview(b"abc")

# min(): Returns the smallest item
min(1, 5, 3)

# next(): Retrieves the next item from an iterator
next(iterator)

# object(): Returns a new featureless object
object()

# oct(): Converts integer to octal string
oct(8)

# open(): Opens a file and returns a stream
open("file.txt")

# ord(): Returns an integer representing the Unicode char
ord("A")

# pow(): Returns base to the power exp
pow(2, 3)

# print(): Prints to the standard output
print("Hello")

# property(): Returns a property attribute
@property

# range(): Generates a sequence of numbers
list(range(5))

# repr(): Returns a string containing printable representation
repr("test")

# reversed(): Returns a reverse iterator
list(reversed([1, 2]))

# round(): Rounds a number
round(3.1415, 2)

# set(): Creates a set
set([1, 2, 2])

# setattr(): Sets the value of an attribute
setattr(obj, "name", value)

# slice(): Returns a slice object representing a set of indices
slice(1, 5, 2)

# sorted(): Returns a new sorted list
sorted([3, 1, 2])

# staticmethod(): Converts a method to a static method
@staticmethod

# str(): Returns a string version of an object
str(10)

# sum(): Sums the items of an iterable
sum([1, 2, 3])

# super(): Returns a proxy object that delegates method calls to a parent or sibling class
super()

# tuple(): Creates a tuple
tuple([1, 2])

# type(): Returns the type of an object
type(5)

# vars(): Returns the __dict__ attribute for a module, class, instance
vars(obj)

# zip(): Iterates over several iterables in parallel
list(zip([1], [2]))

# __import__(): Invoked by the import statement (rarely used directly)
__import__("math")
